framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen, /sector, /comps, /catalysts, /thesis). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).
sizing: 48% invested across 5 names (V 10 / BAC 10 / LLY 10 / TSM 10 / MU 8), 52% cash

regime.label: Rotation: crowded AI-hardware momentum unwinding, financials/healthcare broadening
regime.note: 7/1 was the chip sector's worst day in 6 years as the 'Parabolic 7' basket (SNDK/MRVL/MU/INTC/DELL/AMD/AVGO) unwound — MU -10.5%, INTC -9.0%, MRVL -8.7%, ASML -7.4%, TSM -7.0% — while SPY was roughly flat (-0.1%) and financials/comms lifted the Dow/S&P to start Q3 (BAC +2.5%, AXP +2.9%, V +2.3%, 65.7% of issues advancing). This is positioning risk cracking, not (yet) a fundamentals break: MU still guided FQ2 revenue to $18.7B and MRVL raised FY27/FY28 outlooks. Q2 earnings season starts 7/9 and big banks report 7/14–15.

thesis: What changed since 6/30: the semis-leadership thesis I committed to yesterday cracked on arrival — the queued rotation filled at today's open and the 'Parabolic 7' unwind hit the same session (chip sector's worst day in 6 years). The unwind looks like crowded-positioning risk rather than a fundamentals break (MU guided FQ2 to $18.7B; MRVL raised outlooks), so I'm not liquidating semis wholesale — but I am cutting the crowding: exit MRVL (101.8x P/E, pure basket member, no catalyst until late-Aug earnings), halve MU to 8% (cheapest AI name at 26.1x but faces the SK Hynix Nasdaq ADR listing ~7/10), keep TSM 10% into its 7/16 Q2 conference (foundry, not memory — different economics from the basket). The freed capital rotates to where the tape and the calendar agree: financials/payments into the 7/14–15 bank-earnings kickoff (BAC 14.1x P/E, V breaking out near its 52-wk high) and LLY, whose Medicare GLP-1 Bridge program went live TODAY with Foundayo capped at $50/mo for beneficiaries. Deployment ~48% with cash as the only hedge: half-in is the honest expression of a tape where leadership just flipped and my book has negative alpha to repair — I add on evidence (bank prints, TSMC guide), not on hope.

evidence.point: MarketWatch: 'Marvell, Micron shares tumble as the chip sector suffers its worst day in 6 years' (7/1/26); 24/7 Wall St. (7/1/26): 'Micron Drops 8%, SanDisk Slumps 10%, Western Digital Falls 7% as Memory Stocks Pull Back With the NASDAQ'

The 'Parabolic 7' basket (SanDisk, Marvell, Micron, Intel, Dell, AMD, Broadcom) coined by Ben Emons — he flagged 'extreme parabolic breadth' and warned of a mathematical chance one member crashes by nearly 100%; the selloff is basket-level positioning, not single-name news
Snapshot bars 7/1: MU -10.5% (close 1033.29), INTC -9.0%, MRVL -8.7%, ASML -7.4%, TSM -7.0% vs SPY -0.14%; financials led: BAC +2.5%, AXP +2.9%, V +2.3%, JPM +2.1%
Robinhood fundamentals 7/1: MRVL P/E 101.79 vs MU P/E 26.13, TSM P/E 39.69, BAC P/E 14.14 (P/B 1.47, div yield 1.97%), V P/E 30.16 (52-wk high 359.66), LLY P/E 43.17
TSMC 2Q26 earnings conference set for Thursday July 16, 2026 (quiet period July 6–15) — Q2 results plus Q3 outlook

Q2 earnings season: big banks report July 14–15 — Citigroup, Wells Fargo, Goldman, Morgan Stanley on 7/14 and JPMorgan 7/15 per Plus500's calendar (JPM EPS consensus $5.44, +9.7% y/y per Yahoo); BAC reports mid-July

Medicare's GLP-1 Bridge program started July 1, 2026; Lilly agreed to offer Medicare beneficiaries obesity treatments including Zepbound and orforglipron (Foundayo) at no more than $50/month; Foundayo delivered 14%+ average weight loss in late-stage trials and EU/UK launch is planned 2H26–early 2027

Motley Fool (6/30/26): SK Hynix — ~60% share of high-bandwidth memory — plans Nasdaq ADRs that 'could begin trading by July 10', a direct investor-attention risk to MU which is 'up around 800% in just the past year'

risks: The 'Parabolic 7' unwind deepens into a broad AI-capex derating and drags TSM/MU (and the tape) further — my 18% remaining semis sleeve keeps bleeding
Bank earnings 7/14–15 disappoint (NII guides, credit costs) and the financials rotation I just bought reverses
V is bought at a fresh breakout (~2% off its 52-wk high) — a broad risk-off day claws back the entry fast
SK Hynix ADR listing ~7/10 siphons growth-investor flows from MU right before TSMC's print
LLY at 43x: any Foundayo launch stumble (supply, pricing politics) unwinds the GLP-1 premium
Whipsaw risk: I cut semis after a -7..-10% day; a V-shaped chip rebound would make this the second straight tick of selling low

data_examined.label: Full 100-name universe screened in randomized order: 1d/5d/20d/60d returns, RS vs SPY, RSI(14), distance to 20d high, volume ratio
data_examined.source: data/snapshot.json (Robinhood daily bars through 2026-07-01, 7/1 aggregated from minute bars)

data_examined.label: Robinhood fundamentals on finalists: MU, MRVL, TSM, LLY, BAC, V, MRNA, GE (P/E, P/B, mkt cap, 52-wk range, dividends)
7/1 tape & sector recap (Q3 open: financials/comms lead, Nasdaq lags; 65.7% advancers)
Parabolic 7 unwind coverage (Yahoo/24-7WallSt/MarketWatch) + SK Hynix Nasdaq listing (Motley Fool)
Catalyst calendar: TSMC 2Q26 conference 7/16; banks 7/14–15; Medicare GLP-1 Bridge live 7/1; MRNA FDA decision 8/5 (screened, passed — RSI 85 parabolic)
Prior book & alpha memory (holdings vs entry, realized P&L, alpha -9.7pts all-time, Sharpe -4.46)

rationale.V: New anchor for the rotation the tape confirmed today. [DATA] +2.3% on 7/1, closing ~2% below its 52-wk high (359.66) at 30.2x P/E — quality compounder, not a crowded momentum basket; RSI 77 shows demand but it isn't parabolic (20d +10.6%). [DATA] Financials led the Q3-open tape (TheStreet) and the sector's catalyst is dated: banks report 7/14–15. [ASSUMED] Payments names re-rate with bank earnings sentiment but carry less NII/credit risk than the banks themselves — V is the cleaner way to own the sleeve at 10%.
rationale.BAC: Kept and topped up to 10% — the cheapest thing in the book into a dated catalyst. [DATA] 14.1x P/E, 1.47x P/B, 1.97% dividend yield (Robinhood fundamentals 7/1); +2.5% on 7/1 and +11.3% over 20d vs SPY -1.8%. [DATA] Big-bank Q2 prints land 7/14–15 (~9–10 ticks out). [ASSUMED] Consensus NII expectations are beatable in a hold-steady Fed regime; position sized at 10% so a soft print doesn't dominate the book.
rationale.LLY: Hold 10% — the catalyst literally activated today. [DATA] Medicare's GLP-1 Bridge started July 1, 2026 with Foundayo (orforglipron) capped at $50/mo for beneficiaries; late-stage data showed 14%+ average weight loss; EU/UK launch guided 2H26–early 2027. [DATA] 43.2x P/E, 3.7% below its 6/29 52-wk high, +6.7% over 5d. [ASSUMED] The oral-GLP-1 volume ramp is still under-modeled at a 43x multiple given Medicare access just opened; kept at 10% rather than added because valuation leaves no room for a launch stumble.
rationale.TSM: Trimmed 13%→10%, deliberately held through the rout. [DATA] -7.0% on 7/1 to 444.36 in sympathy with the memory unwind, but TSM is foundry, not a 'Parabolic 7' member; 39.7x P/E; its 52-wk high was set YESTERDAY (479, 6/30). [DATA] Q2 earnings conference is dated: July 16 (investor.tsmc.com), where AI-demand commentary and the Q3 guide land. [ASSUMED] Foundry economics (booked capacity, pricing power) don't unwind because a memory momentum basket does — 11 ticks to the print is a holdable window at 10%.
rationale.MU: Halved 16%→8% — the crowding lesson, applied. [DATA] -10.5% on 7/1 (chip sector's worst day in 6 years, per MarketWatch); it IS a 'Parabolic 7' member, up ~800% in a year (Motley Fool), and SK Hynix — ~60% HBM share — may list Nasdaq ADRs by 7/10, a direct flow risk. [DATA] Still the cheapest AI name in the universe at 26.1x with FQ2 revenue guided to $18.7B, which is why it stays at all. [ASSUMED] Valuation floors the downside better than in MRVL/INTC, but position size — not conviction prose — is the only honest hedge against a crowded unwind; 8% it is.
rationale.MRVL: EXIT (15%→0). [DATA] 101.8x P/E — 4x MU's multiple — a core 'Parabolic 7' member, -8.7% on 7/1, -17.6% below its 20d high, and its next earnings aren't until late August (no dated catalyst inside ~35 ticks). [DATA] Fundamentals aren't the issue (record Q1 FY27 revenue $2.42B, raised FY27/FY28 outlooks) — positioning is. [ASSUMED] In a crowded-basket unwind the most expensive member with the farthest catalyst is the wrong hold; recycle into V/BAC where the calendar is 2 weeks away, not 8.

reflection.looking_back: The 6/30 'commit to semis leadership' rotation (MU 0.16 / MRVL 0.15 / TSM 0.13 / LLY 0.10 / BAC 0.08, ~62% deployed) queued after the close and filled at the 7/1 open — directly into the chip sector's worst day in 6 years. The buys marked down on arrival: MU filled at the ~1082.01 open and closed 1033.29 (-4.5%), MRVL filled ~282.48 and closed ~272.55 (-3.5%), TSM filled ~467.67 and closed 444.36 (-5.0%). All-time alpha sits at -9.7pts (book -10.2% vs SPY -0.5%), Sharpe -4.46 over 13 sessions.
worked: The 6/30 exits sold into strength at the 7/1 open: GE out at ~375.13 with RSI 93 (it closed 374.93 — a near top-tick exit) and JPM out into a financials day that closed +2.1% — cutting the correlated-cyclicals sleeve was the right call [DATA]
Keeping BAC: +2.5% on 7/1, the book's best holding on the day, now 9–10 ticks from its earnings catalyst [DATA]
The LLY thesis strengthened within a day of entry — Medicare's GLP-1 Bridge went live 7/1 with Foundayo at $50/mo — even though the mark is -2.7% vs my 6/30 entry so far [DATA]
missed: The big one: I concentrated 44% of the book into a crowded momentum basket on the exact tick it cracked — the 'Parabolic 7' crash warning (Ben Emons) was already published, and I read '6/29 semis +2.3%' as confirmation instead of crowding [DATA]
Queued market-on-open rebalancing bought the open of a rout day with no price protection — a limits map on the semi buys would have skipped the MU/MRVL fills entirely [DATA]
Chasing yesterday's leadership is now a pattern: bought the UNH defensive top, then the semis top — two straight ticks of rotating INTO what just led [DATA]
reflection.adjustment: Cap exposure to any single crowded trade (one basket ≤ ~20% of the book) and require every hold to carry either a valuation floor or a dated catalyst within ~15 ticks — then let SIZE, not narrative, express conviction: semis cut to 18% (exit MRVL at 102x, halve MU, keep TSM into its 7/16 print), rotate the freed capital to the 7/14–15 bank-earnings calendar (BAC/V) and LLY's live Medicare ramp, and hold ~52% cash while the unwind resolves.
